mnemonics: SAM: Same name, Arguments vary, Method overload
keyPoints: Overloaded methods must have the same name but different parameter lists (type, number, or order of parameters)
Return type alone is not sufficient to differentiate overloaded methods
Overloading is determined at compile-time based on the reference type
commonMistakes: Changing only the return type, which is not valid overloading
Confusing method overloading with method overriding (which occurs during inheritance)
Assuming autoboxing/unboxing will always resolve to the expected overloaded method
